title: Darkness Problems Not the DVDs Fault
display_title: Darkness Problems Not the DVDs Fault
wikitext:
[]: Darkness Problems: Not the DVD's Fault             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 23, 2008 5:08 pm The current consensus is that the DVD release (1.01) ruined a lot of the video quality by making certain scenes way too dark and thus removing a lot of detail. For example, here's a comparison of a shot from the "Fly Me to the Moon" music video as compared to the same shot from 1.01: I completely agree that the shot was made much, much worse by the fact that it was darkened so much. However, the darkening was not done specifically for the updated 1.01 release (a.k.a. the DVD release). It was just as dark in theaters. In other words, the scene was ruined in the theatrical release as well as the DVD release. I don't have any proof, but I have a bunch of hard evidence. EXHIBIT A: The "Fly Me to the Moon" music video uses obsolete animation that was changed before the film was shown theatrically . Certain mistakes present in the FMttM video were fixed not for 1.01, but for the theatrical release of 1.0. For example, the shot during Operation Yashima in which you see a closeup of Shinji's eye which zooms out to a full-body nude (with no genitalia, of course): Pay careful attention to the black stripe on the right side of Shinji's plugsuit closest to Shinji's neck (our right; his left). It jumps pretty badly over the course of these two consecutive frames. This is the worst problem that I noticed in this zoom-out, but there are several others. Overall, the whole shot was pretty problematic. Anno/Khara must have felt the same way, as the shot was redone by the time the film was released: During this frame (and all previous frames), the stripe was fixed. Several other changes were made as well, such as the fact that the shot starts out with much more color, the little spike thing is no longer visible on Shinji's right shoulder, the nose bridge is-- well, the whole thing was pretty much redrawn. These changes aren't limited to this one scene, but I chose this one as an example as the changes are the most prominent. In fact, the stripe bothered me the first time I saw it. How can I prove that the animation in this shot was redone before the film was released theatrically? Why, with the crappy camrip, of course! This is the exact same frame as in 1.01, though it's kind of hard to tell due to the fact that it's all distorted and zoomed in because it was kinescoped from the movie screen. Anyway, this proves that the footage shown in the "Fly Me to the Moon" music video is not the final footage. So, this means that it's quite likely that other things were changed by the time 1.0 was released. I can prove that certain details were changed, but proving that the lighting was changed is much more difficult, as the camrip's colors and detail level are all terrible anyway. However, it is still possible for me to show that the Sachiel fight was darkened after the "Fly Me to the Moon" video was released and before the theatrical version was released. I present... EXHIBIT B: This shot is still darker in the final version than it was in the "Fly Me to the Moon" video, but it wasn't darkened nearly as much as the Sachiel fight. To put it simply, in the "Fly Me to the Moon" video, the shot of Unit 00 rising from the ground has approximately the same brightness level as the Sachiel fight. However, in the final version, the shot of Unit 00 rising from the ground is much brighter than the Sachiel fight. This is obviously the case on the 1.01 DVD, but was it already this way in the theatrical showing of 1.0? Yes. Yes it was. The camrip's crappiness actually HELPS prove this. In the camrip, it is pretty much impossible to make out anything that is too dark. You can still make out a good amount of detail in Unit 00's rise: However, it is pretty much impossible to make out anything except Unit 01's glowiness during the Sachiel fight: Therefore, the scene must have been darkened before the theatrical release of 1.0. It's not the DVD's fault. This doesn't excuse the DVD from being all grainy and wobbly, of course, nor does it excuse the poor decision Khara made to darken the Sachiel fight (as well as other scenes) so miserably. OK, Reichu, NOW DO YOU BELIEVE ME?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 23, 2008 5:14 pm It's like your presentation ended just when it was getting started.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 23, 2008 5:14 pm Yes, my sponsor cut my budget and told me they didn't like my ending. I had a very short time to redo it and had to make do with what little resources I had.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:14 pm I personally like the darker look. You may lose some details but personally I don't care. It feels more filmic in that version. The highly bright trailer images are very pretty no doubt, but they look highly digitized and too slick for me. But that's just me, I prefer more gritty tones than slick pastels. So it doesn't bug me. And is it really the popular opinion when only the same three people have complained about it? I mean I know those three people are responsible for 51% of the posts on this board but that doesn't make it the general one. That's my opinion and everyone's entitled to their own. I know the trailers are more shiny but I like the darker, grittier DVD look.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:24 pm I wasn't aware anything in this thread even alluded to the concept that a majority of people here hate the movie's "LOL DRAMATIC DARKNESS". So why bring it up (except to be a douche, I guess) ? :p That said, NAW hasn't convinced me of the validity of his thesis yet, but the whole "let's make some scenes so dark you can't see anything" deal is totally idiotic nonetheless. If you disagree, you are wrong. (Or, at least, you're not the sort of person who likes to be able to see all of the detail that goes into a production like this.)

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:38 pm Agree to disagree is all I'm saying.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Nov 23, 2008 6:50 pm Reminds me of the infamous red-tint on the region 2 "Spirited Away" DVDs. Just from looking at the pictures, it is indeed darker. That's annoying. Way to go Anno, who recommended you do this, Matt Greenfield?

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Nov 24, 2008 4:47 am What really sucks with that brightness problem is that even lights are darkened. It really doesn't look normal.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Nov 24, 2008 5:49 am Darkness is generally bad... makes things almost as hard to follow as the dreaded shaky cam. I also really don't like the added neon green to 01 (wish they replaced all the green and orange crap with more of the chestplate blue/gray and and maybe a hint of lavender personally) but the fact that that's basically all you can see in the first fight is really a let down. Unit 01 is not at a rave, damn it!
